truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 against 20 ground-truth vulnerabilities. Run 2 correctly identified 8 vulnerabilities but missed 12. Several findings had incorrect severity ratings or mischaracterized vulnerability types (e.g., mislabeling Use-After-Free as a memory leak). One finding was not in the ground truth.

Items.Notes: Correctly identified the runtime format string passed to printf.
Integer overflow in the factorial function was not reported.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Memory Leak in Deferred History Flush
Items.Notes: Reported as a resource leak instead of identifying the Use-After-Free condition during pool cleanup.
Correctly identified system() call with concatenated, unvalidated config values.
Correctly identified the unbounded strcpy and off-by-one in safe_string_copy.
Correctly identified the read-before-lock race condition.
Logic bomb bypass using EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE was not reported.
Path traversal via config_dir_ + filename was not reported.
Correctly identified strcpy into a fixed 256-byte expression_buffer_.
Correctly identified the hardcoded ADMIN_SECRET constant.
Weak PRNG (mt19937) used for session tokens was not reported.
Thread-unsafe static cached_time in logging was not reported.
Insecure temporary file handling via system() was not reported.
Persistent authentication state issues were not reported.
Unhandled exception from std::stod in variable parsing was not reported.
Integer underflow during memory pool block splitting was not reported.
Unbounded recursion risk in the parser was not reported.
Data race in the result cache was not reported.
TOCTOU race between filesystem::exists and file open was not reported.
Correctly identified division by zero in calculate_route_score.

Items.Key: command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|608|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|an authenticated user can execute arbitrary operating system commands with the privileges of the running process.|user sends a command starting with 'exec ' followed by malicious shell syntax after authentication.|avoid system() for user input. use a strict allowlist of commands or invoke binaries via execve/fork with sanitized, fixed arguments.->command injection in admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|608|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str()); }|an authenticated user can execute arbitrary operating system commands with the privileges of the running process.|user sends a command starting with 'exec ' followed by malicious shell syntax after authentication.|avoid system() for user input. use a strict allowlist of commands or invoke binaries via execve/fork with sanitized, fixed arguments.

buffer overflow in evaluate_expression|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|834|supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); }|when input length is >= 1024, strcpy writes past input_buffer_ without bounds checking, risking memory corruption.|providing an expression string with length greater than or equal to 1024 bytes.|apply safe_string_copy or truncation in the >= branch instead of using unbounded strcpy.->buffer overflow in evaluate_expression|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|0.95|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|834|supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); }|when input length is >= 1024, strcpy writes past input_buffer_ without bounds checking, risking memory corruption.|providing an expression string with length greater than or equal to 1024 bytes.|apply safe_string_copy or truncation in the >= branch instead of using unbounded strcpy.
